THE LIFE OF A ROBBER NAMED CYRIACOS somebody came and knocked at the door. The elder went out and opened to him. Then he took out and set before him bread and The <same?> friend of Christ* told us about a robber named soaked <peas>, which he wolfed down. I thought it must be some Cyriacos whose thefts were committed in the area around Emus, stranger; I looked through the window and saw hat it was a lion. known also as Nicopolis. He became so cruel and inhuman that 1 said to the elder: ‘Good elder, why do you feed that animal? they called him ‘the wolf". There were other robbers with him; not Explain to me’. He said: ‘I have required of it that it harm neither only Christians, but also Judaeans and Samaritans too. One day—it man nor beast; and I have told it to come here each day and I will was in holy week—some people from an estate in the region of give it its food. It has come twice a day now for seven months— Nicopolis came up to the Holy City to baptise their children. When and I feed it." Some days later I met him again, when I wanted to <the children> had been baptised, they were returning 10 their own buy some bottles for he occupied his hands by making botUes. T estate to celebrate Easter Day there. But they were confronted by said to him: “How are things, good elder! How is the lion? He the robbers on the way (the chieftain was not present). The men answered: ‘Badly’, and 1 asked: "How so?” He told me: ‘It came here took to their hecls. Casting aside the newly baptised <children>, the to be fed yesterday and T noticed that its muzzle was ali stained with Hebrews and Samaritans seized the women and took possession of blood. 1 said to it: “What is this? You have disobeyed me and eaten them, As the men (led, they ran into the chieftain of the robbers flesh. Blessed Lord! Never again will I feed you the food of the who asked them: ‘Why are you running away? They told him what fathers, carnivore! Get away from here.” He would nol go away, so had happened to them.
